writing_style.context: I write in a warm, direct tone that balances clinical precision with genuine human warmth. My bullet points always start with a strong action verb and include a quantified outcome where possible. I avoid acronyms unless they are universally understood in the field and never use passive voice. I frame accomplishments in terms of patient or guest impact rather than internal processes.

profiles.label: Traveling Nurse — Phlebotomy & Immunizations
profiles.summary: Bilingual Registered Nurse with 20 years of experience across hospital systems in France and the United States, now specializing as a traveling phlebotomist and immunization specialist. Comfortable operating independently in mobile clinic settings, corporate wellness days, and rural outreach programs. Committed to making preventive care accessible and stress-free for every patient.

profiles.label: Culinary Apprentice — Michelin-Star Kitchen
profiles.summary: Self-taught and formally trained cook with nearly two decades of event catering experience in Paris and an entrepreneurial track record in the US meal-prep market. Seeking a structured apprenticeship in a Michelin-starred kitchen to formalize classical French technique, develop professional brigade discipline, and ultimately earn a place as a line cook in fine dining. Brings unusual strengths in precision, composure under pressure, and rigorous food-safety practice — all honed in a healthcare career.

summary: Bilingual registered nurse and passionate cook with two decades of cross-continental experience, bringing clinical precision and creative adaptability to every setting.

experience.job_title: Staff Nurse, General Medicine & Infectious Disease
bullets: Provided bedside nursing care for a 32-bed general medicine unit seeing 1,400+ patient admissions per year
Performed daily phlebotomy rounds covering 28–35 patients, achieving a first-stick success rate above 97%
Administered IV medications, blood transfusions, and subcutaneous immunizations according to physician orders
Mentored five newly qualified nurses through the French hospital orientation program (DPC)
Served as elected staff representative in biannual union–management negotiations, securing improved night-shift rest periods
Coordinated interdisciplinary discharge planning with social workers, physiotherapists, and general practitioners
Planned and executed full menus for 30 to 180-guest private dinners, corporate receptions, and outdoor garden parties
Developed rotating seasonal menus in close collaboration with a certified sommelier to pair courses with AOC wines
Sourced ingredients directly from Rungis wholesale market twice weekly, negotiating quality produce at 15–20% below retail
Led a kitchen team of up to four assistants during events, applying brigade-style task delegation to keep service on schedule
Received consistent 5-star client ratings on La Belle Assiette with a 78% repeat-booking rate
Catered a 120-guest reception at the Musée d'Orsay for a private cultural foundation gala in 2010
Triaged and managed care for up to 220 daily patient visits in a Level I Trauma Center emergency department
Initiated and maintained peripheral IV lines, central line monitoring, and arterial blood gas draws under physician supervision
Administered influenza, tetanus-diphtheria, and hepatitis B vaccines as part of the hospital preventive care protocol
Achieved ACLS certification and served as first responder in 14 documented code-blue events with 100% protocol adherence
Rotated as Charge Nurse, managing a team of 11 nurses and 4 patient care technicians across 8-hour shifts
Precepted 8 new graduate nurses across three cohorts
Founded a meal-prep subscription service delivering 200+ weekly portions of chef-prepared, nutritionally balanced meals to New York families
Designed rotating 4-week menus rooted in French bistro technique — ratatouille confit, poulet rôti, bouillabaisse-style stews — adapted for batch production
Scaled from solo operation to a 3-person kitchen team within 18 months, with revenue reaching $180,000 in year two
Maintained an A-grade NYC Department of Health inspection rating across all six inspection cycles
Relocated operations to Seattle in 2020, pivoting to a ghost-kitchen model serving 140 weekly subscribers through a local delivery cooperative
Managed food cost at 28% of revenue through yield tracking, batch purchasing, and strategic menu engineering
Delivered intensive critical care in a 14-bed mixed medical-surgical ICU serving a rural community of 35,000
Managed complex patient caseloads including post-operative cardiac, respiratory failure, and multi-organ dysfunction cases
Performed arterial line draws, central line blood cultures, and timed therapeutic drug monitoring specimens
Collaborated with physicians, respiratory therapists, and pharmacists during daily multidisciplinary rounds
Reduced catheter-associated bloodstream infection (CLABSI) rates by 40% through a peer-led protocol compliance initiative
Led annual flu shot clinic, vaccinating 380+ community residents and hospital staff over two days
Deploy to 3–5 clinical sites per week: corporate wellness fairs, assisted-living facilities, rural health clinics, and school vaccination days
Collect an average of 65 blood specimens per shift with a sustained first-stick success rate of 98.3%
Administer influenza, COVID-19, pneumococcal, shingles (Zoster), and travel vaccines across diverse patient populations
Maintain the mobile clinic vehicle — stock rotation, cold-chain compliance, and instrument calibration — to CLIA waiver standards
Serve bilingual patients in English and French, reducing appointment anxiety in francophone patients
Completed WA State DOH standing orders training for expanded vaccine administration without per-patient physician orders

projects.name: Bistro at Home
bullets: Write weekly long-form recipes translating classical French brasserie dishes into approachable weeknight meals
Grew readership to 14,000 monthly unique visitors and 9,200 newsletter subscribers without paid promotion
Partnered with two artisan French cheese importers for sponsored recipe series on cave-aged AOC cheeses

Distributed to 60+ colleagues in the Northwest Mobile Health network; adopted as onboarding reading by the clinical director

education.school: Institut de Formation en Soins Infirmiers — CHU de Lyon
education.notes: 3-year full-time program including 2,100 clinical placement hours across general medicine, surgery, pediatrics, and psychiatry. Equivalent to a BSN under French-US credential recognition.
Two-semester evening program completed alongside full-time nursing. Covered classical sauce-making, butchery, pastry foundations, and à la minute plating.

awards.title: Nurse Excellence Award
awards.description: Awarded annually to one nurse per department for outstanding patient care and peer mentorship; nominated by colleagues and selected by the Chief Nursing Officer.

awards.title: Employee of the Month
awards.description: Recognized for coordinating patient care during a mass-casualty event in the 2015 blizzard while serving as Charge Nurse on duty.

awards.title: Coup de Cœur — Amateur Catering Category
awards.description: Annual competition open to non-professional cooks. Awarded for a 5-course autumn menu featuring Île-de-France produce, including a venison consommé with black truffle and a tarte Tatin revisitée.

publications.title: First-Stick Success in Challenging Populations: A Traveling Nurse's Field Notes
publications.publisher: Journal of Infusion Nursing — Continuing Education Section
publications.description: Practical overview of patient positioning, vein visualization techniques, and psychological de-escalation for anxious patients during difficult blood draws. 1,200-word peer-reviewed contribution.

bullets: Staff community blood drives across the Seattle metro area as a licensed volunteer phlebotomist
Guide first-time donors through the screening and collection process, minimizing adverse reactions through calm reassurance
Prepare and serve hot meals for 80–120 transitional housing residents monthly
Develop seasonal menus that maximize nutrition and flavor within a $3-per-head food budget
